偎.origin: 偎 means to cuddle up close to someone you love.

sentence.en: The kitten snuggles up to its mother.

偏.origin: 偏 means slanted or to one side, not in the middle.

sentence.en: Mom will not play favorites.

偕.origin: 偕 means together or accompanied by, going along with someone.

sentence.en: He came together with his wife.

健.origin: 健 shows a person 亻 who can build 建 things; it means strong and healthy.

sentence.en: Wishing you good health.

偶.origin: 偶 means by chance, or a doll or puppet shaped like a person.

sentence.en: I occasionally go to the park.

偷.origin: 偷 means to steal, taking what is not yours.

sentence.en: Do not steal other people's things.

偿.origin: 偿 means to repay or pay back what is owed.

sentence.en: He repaid the money he borrowed.

傅.origin: 傅 means a teacher or tutor; it is also a common surname.

sentence.en: The master teaches me to cook.

傍.origin: 傍 means beside or close to, near someone's side 旁.

sentence.en: At dusk the sky grows dark.

储.origin: 储 means to save up or store things for later.

sentence.en: Squirrels store pine cones.

催.origin: 催 means to urge or hurry someone along.

sentence.en: Mom urges me to get up quickly.

傲.origin: 傲 means proud or haughty, looking down on others.

sentence.en: I am proud of you.
Don't be a fool.
A monk lives on the mountain.

僵.origin: 僵 means stiff and unable to move.

sentence.en: My hands went stiff from the cold.

儿.origin: 儿 is the simple form of 兒, a picture of a child; it means son or child.

sentence.en: She has a son.

兀.origin: 兀 shows a flat top above legs 儿; it means bare or towering.

sentence.en: The peak looks very abrupt.

兄.origin: 兄 shows a mouth 口 above legs 儿; it means elder brother who speaks for the young.

sentence.en: They are real brothers.

兆.origin: 兆 pictures cracks in oracle bones; it means an omen or a trillion.

sentence.en: This is a good sign.

光.origin: 光 shows a person carrying fire; it means light.

sentence.en: The sunshine is warm.

免.origin: 免 means to avoid or be excused from something.

sentence.en: Entry is free today.

兑.origin: 兑 means to exchange, like changing money or cashing a check.

sentence.en: I go to the bank to exchange money.

党.origin: 党 means a political party or group of people.

sentence.en: This country has many political parties.

兜.origin: 兜 means a pouch or pocket that holds things.

sentence.en: He put the candy in his pocket.

公.origin: 公 means fair or public, sharing 八 what was private 厶.

sentence.en: We go to the park to play.

兰.origin: 兰 pictures an orchid in bloom.

sentence.en: There is a pot of orchids on the table.

共.origin: 共 shows two hands holding one thing; it means together or total.

sentence.en: There are five of us in total.

兵.origin: 兵 shows hands holding a weapon; it means a soldier.

sentence.en: The soldiers are protecting the city.

其.origin: 其 means that or its, pointing to something already mentioned.

sentence.en: I like one of them.

兹.origin: 兹 means now or here, often used in formal writing.

sentence.en: It is hereby set that the meeting is tomorrow.

兼.origin: 兼 pictures a hand holding two grains; it means both at once.

sentence.en: He does a part-time job after school.

兽.origin: 兽 pictures an animal with horns, eyes, and mouth; it means beast.

sentence.en: There are wild beasts in the forest.

冀.origin: 冀 means to hope for; it is also the short name for Hebei.

sentence.en: I hope tomorrow will be better.

冈.origin: 冈 is the simple form of 岡; it means the ridge of a hill.

sentence.en: The sheep graze on the hill.

册.origin: 册 pictures bamboo strips tied together to make a book.

sentence.en: This is a picture album.

冒.origin: 冒 means to brave or risk, or to give off as in steam.

sentence.en: Steam rises from the pot.

军.origin: 军 pictures soldiers around a cart; it means army.

sentence.en: He is a soldier.

冠.origin: 冠 shows a hand putting a cap on a head; it means crown or hat.

sentence.en: He won the championship.

冤.origin: 冤 means a wrong or injustice done to someone.

sentence.en: You have wronged an innocent person.

冯.origin: 冯 is a common Chinese surname.

sentence.en: My classmate's surname is Feng.

冰.origin: 冰 joins the ice radical 冫 with water 水; it means ice.

sentence.en: In summer I love drinking ice water.

况.origin: 况 means a situation or condition; it also means moreover.

sentence.en: Please tell me the current situation.

冶.origin: 冶 means to smelt or melt metal to shape it.

sentence.en: The factory is smelting steel.

冻.origin: 冻 joins the ice radical 冫 with east 东; it means to freeze.

sentence.en: The fish is frozen in the fridge.

净.origin: 净 means clean, without any dirt.

sentence.en: Wash your hands clean.

凉.origin: 凉 joins the ice radical 冫 with 京; it means cool or cold.

sentence.en: It is nice and cool under the tree.

减.origin: 减 means to take away or subtract, making less.

sentence.en: Please reduce waste.

凑.origin: 凑 means to put together or gather pieces into a whole.

sentence.en: We pooled money to buy a gift.

凝.origin: 凝 means to freeze solid or gaze fixedly.

sentence.en: Water solidifies when it gets cold.

凡.origin: 凡 pictures a flat dish; it means ordinary or every.

sentence.en: He lives an ordinary life.
